name: Sentry Error Monitoring
description: Issues, events, releases, performance via Sentry's official MCP

env_vars.description: Sentry OAuth Access Token (NOT user auth token)
env_vars.how_to_set: Sentry's MCP endpoint requires an OAuth token. Regular auth tokens will NOT work. Create an OAuth application at sentry.io/settings/developer-settings, complete the OAuth flow, then paste the access token. See: docs.sentry.io/api/guides/create-auth-token

env_vars.description: Your Sentry organization slug (found in org URL)
env_vars.how_to_set: sentry.io → Settings → Organization → slug shown in URL

tools.name: list_issues
tools.description: List Sentry error issues for a project with filtering by status, assignee, or date range

project_slug.description: The Sentry project slug to list issues for

limit.description: Maximum number of issues to return (max 100)

tools.name: get_issue
tools.description: Retrieve full details of a Sentry issue including stack trace, user impact, and occurrence count

issue_id.description: The Sentry issue ID to retrieve

tools.name: resolve_issue
tools.description: Mark a Sentry issue as resolved, optionally in the next release or a specific version

issue_id.description: The Sentry issue ID to resolve

status_details.description: Optional resolution details, e.g. inNextRelease: true or inRelease: '1.2.3'

tools.name: list_projects
tools.description: List all Sentry projects in the organization with their slugs, platforms, and team assignments

cursor.description: Pagination cursor for fetching the next page of results

tools.name: get_event
tools.description: Retrieve a specific Sentry event by ID with full exception details, breadcrumbs, and context

issue_id.description: The Sentry issue ID the event belongs to

event_id.description: The specific event ID to retrieve (use 'latest' for the most recent event)
